message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar (La Quinta)
Cuisine/type signal: Japanese sushi / robata
Why it surfaced in discovery: Highway 111 sushi destination cited as the only sushi restaurant in the region with daily Grade A deliveries from Japan; distinctive interior (bronze Buddha, petrified cherry blossom tree)
Discovery sources: https://okurasushi.com/ | https://www.playinlaquinta.com/restaurants/okura-robata-grill-sushi-bar/ | https://www.opentable.com/r/okura-robata-grill-and-sushi-bar-la-quinta

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

Based on the search results, here's information about Okura Robata Grill Sushi Bar in La Quinta from various review platforms:

## Overview
Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar serves up a fusion of Japanese and California cuisine in a chic contemporary restaurant space that has an L.A./Hollywood vibe.

## Ratings
- Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar - La Quinta is rated 4.7 stars by 2979 OpenTable diners.
- Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar in La Quinta, CA, is a popular Japanese restaurant that has earned an average rating of 4.4 stars.

## What Reviewers Praise
Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar in La Quinta is lauded for its "freshest sushi," "beautiful presentation," and "exceptional service." The food is fresh and well prepared in an extremely clean restaurant with friendly/helpful staff.

## Menu & Specialties
Okura is the only sushi restaurant in the region to receive daily deliveries of Grade A fish and seafood are "Grade A" coming in from Japan. Beyond an expansive sushi menu, there is something for everyone including skewers from the robata grill, salads, Kobe steak, tempura and chicken, beef and fish entrees, as well as delicious desserts!

## Location & Reservations
Reservations are recommended for this popular spot. The restaurant is located at 78480 Highway 111 in La Quinta.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best sushi restaurants in the Coachella Valley and Palm Springs area in 2025:

## Top Recommendations

**Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey** - If you're searching for refined, chef-driven sushi in Palm Springs, Sandfish Sushi sets the standard. Helmed by Chef Engin Onural, this sleek, modern restaurant blends traditional Japanese technique with subtle global influence. The omakase experience rotates seasonally based on fish flown in from Japan and the West Coast. The whiskey list is one of the most impressive in the Coachella Valley.

**Ponzu Sushi** - Ponzu Sushi offers a welcoming setting and a broad menu that balances classic sushi staples with creative house rolls. It's especially popular for takeout and relaxed dine-in experiences. Located by the Palm Springs downtown park.

**Taka Shin** - Taka Shin has quietly built a reputation for fresh fish, generous portions, and warm hospitality.

**Otori Sushi** - Tucked away in Cathedral City on Ramon, Otori Sushi is a hidden gem beloved by locals. Known for its generous portions, friendly service, and beautifully plated sushi rolls, this casual yet high-quality spot offers a fantastic selection of both traditional and specialty rolls.

**Happy Sushi** - Happy Sushi lives up to its name with colorful rolls, combo specials, and an upbeat vibe. It's a great option for groups or travelers looking for a satisfying meal before heading out for the night. This casual restaurant is centrally located downtown, in Mercado Plaza by JimmyB's, and offers a large sushi bar.

Three of the five restaurants specialize in sushi: Ponzu Sushi, Otori Sushi, and Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey.

# Sushi Restaurants Featured

No, Okura Robata Grill in La Quinta is not mentioned in this article.

The five sushi restaurants highlighted are:

1. **Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey** — Described as offering "refined, chef-driven sushi" with an impressive whisky selection, ideal for special occasions.

2. **Taka Shin Japanese Cuisine** — A "downtown favorite with loyal locals" known for fresh fish and generous portions.

3. **Joyce's Sushi** — Features "inventive specialty rolls and fresh ingredients" with a neighborhood atmosphere.

4. **Ponzu Sushi** — Characterized as "comfortable, consistent, and crowd-pleasing," popular for takeout and casual dining.

5. **Happy Sushi Palm Springs** — Described as "fun, fast, and full of flavor" with an upbeat vibe, located in Mercado Plaza downtown.

content.content:
# Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar - Key Information

**Location & Contact:**
- Address: 78480 HWY 111, La Quinta, CA 92253
- Phone: (760) 564-5820
- Email: info@okurasushi.com

**Hours of Operation:**
- Mon-Thu: 3:30 PM - 9:00 PM
- Fri-Sat: 3:30 PM - 9:30 PM
- Sunday: 3:30 PM - 9:00 PM
- Happy Hour: Sun-Thu, 3:30 PM - 5:00 PM

**Cuisine & Offerings:**
The establishment describes itself as serving "a fusion of Japanese and California cuisine" featuring sushi, robata grill skewers, Kobe steak, tempura, and various seafood entrees. They claim to be the only regional sushi restaurant receiving daily Grade A fish shipments from Japan.

**Notable Features:**
The venue showcases distinctive décor including gold chain installations and a life-size petrified Japanese cherry blossom tree. The full bar offers signature cocktails and premium sakes.

**Recognition:**
The restaurant has received "Best Sushi & Japanese in the Desert" accolades from local publications.

**Services:**
Online ordering, digital gift cards, catering, and reservation booking available through their website.

# Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar - Review Summary

**Total Reviews:** 251

**Average Rating:** 4.1 out of 5 bubbles

**La Quinta Ranking:** #16 of 127 restaurants

**Recent Sentiment (2025-2026):** Mixed results. A recent April 2025 review praised the "unbelievably fresh sushi" and "beautifully decorated" interior, while acknowledging it is "Expensive, yes, but a wonderful restaurant." However, an August 2023 review noted poor service during a birthday dinner, with complaints about rushed service and lack of dessert offerings.

**Awards Mentioned:** The restaurant displays a Travelers' Choice award badge, indicating it "consistently earn[s] great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties on Tripadvisor."

**Key Themes:** Reviews consistently highlight fresh sushi quality and attractive ambiance, though service inconsistency and crowding during peak seasons emerge as recurring concerns.

Web search results for query: ""Okura" La Quinta sushi 2025 review"

Links: [{"title":"OKURA ROBATA GRILL & SUSHI BAR, La Quinta - Restaurant Reviews, Photos & Reservations - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32584-d638998-Reviews-Okura_Robata_Grill_Sushi_Bar-La_Quinta_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar - La Quinta Restaurant - La Quinta, CA | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/r/okura-robata-grill-and-sushi-bar-la-quinta"},{"title":"OKURA ROBATA GRILL AND SUSHI BAR - Updated September 2025 - 1493 Photos & 1398 Reviews - 78480 Hwy 111, La Quinta, California - Japanese - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Menu - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/okura-robata-grill-and-sushi-bar-la-quinta?start=520"},{"title":"Go for Happy Hour! - Review of Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar, La Quinta, CA - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/ShowUserReviews-g32584-d638998-r134226446-Okura_Robata_Grill_and_Sushi_Bar_La_Quinta-La_Quinta_Greater_Palm_Springs_Californ.html"},{"title":"Home - Best Sushi Desert - Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar","url":"https://okurasushi.com/"},{"title":"Okura La Quinta: Experience Okura Sushi in La Quinta","url":"https://www.playinlaquinta.com/restaurants/okura-robata-grill-sushi-bar/"},{"title":"Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar Restaurant La Quinta Palm Springs CA Reviews | GAYOT","url":"https://www.gayot.com/restaurants/okura-robata-grill-sushi-bar-la-quinta-ca-92253_35ps060502.html"},{"title":"Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar Reviews","url":"https://www.grubhub.com/restaurant/okura-robata-grill--sushi-bar-78480-ca-111-la-quinta/2106677/reviews"},{"title":"kitchen - Picture of Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar, La Quinta - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g32584-d638998-i118487811-Okura_Robata_Grill_and_Sushi_Bar_La_Quinta-La_Quinta_Greater_Palm_Springs_.html"},{"title":"My friend's sushi with salmon - she loved it! - Picture of Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar, La Quinta - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g32584-d638998-i463905959-Okura_Robata_Grill_and_Sushi_Bar_La_Quinta-La_Quinta_Greater_Palm_Springs_.html"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar in La Quinta:

## Overview
Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar serves up a fusion of Japanese and California cuisine in a chic contemporary restaurant space that has an L.A./Hollywood vibe. Okura is the only sushi restaurant in the region to receive daily deliveries of Grade A fish and seafood coming in from Japan.

## Recent Reviews (2025)
The restaurant is rated 4.7 stars by 2,979 OpenTable diners. Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar in La Quinta is lauded for its "freshest sushi," "beautiful presentation," and "exceptional service." Frequent visitors appreciate the variety for non-sushi lovers and the consistent high quality.

## Ambiance & Dining
Custom décor includes gold Kriskadecor chains hanging from the ceiling, a 4-foot high bronze Buddha and a life-size, petrified Japanese cherry blossom tree at the center of the restaurant. Reservations are recommended for this popular spot.

## Menu & Service
Beyond an expansive sushi menu, there is something for everyone including skewers from the Robata Grill, Salads, Toban Yaki Steak, Tempura, Chicken, Beef and Fish Entrees, and of course, delicious desserts. The food is fresh and well prepared in an extremely clean restaurant with friendly/helpful staff.

Based on the web search results, here are some excellent restaurant options in Old Town La Quinta for a date night, including sushi options:

## Best Date Night Restaurants

**Lavender Bistro** offers a world class Contemporary American menu with an outdoor patio featuring fresh flowers, sparkling lights, and live music. It's been voted "Best Romantic Restaurant" by Open Table.

**Lola's of the Cove** is a globally inspired, locally rooted dining destination where thoughtfully crafted plates and refined cocktails come together in an intimate, inviting setting designed for lingering evenings and shared moments.

## Sushi Options

For sushi specifically in the La Quinta area:

1. **Coral Sushi & Sake Bar** offers amazing fresh sushi, sashimi, specialty rolls and Asian inspired entrees in a casual and relaxed setting with a trendy, vibrant atmosphere and an outdoor covered patio.

2. **Misaki Sushi & Grill** has an extensive menu with everything from classic rolls to their Sashimi Special (a Chef's Choice of their freshest fish).

3. **Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar** serves a fusion of both traditional Japanese and California-style sushi, with prices on the higher end but receiving daily deliveries of Grade A fish and seafood.

Old Town La Quinta's restaurants offer some of the Coachella Valley's most inspired places to dine while enjoying the charm of Old Town, with intimate and casual atmospheres.

Based on the search results, here are some excellent sushi and Japanese restaurant recommendations near Palm Desert and La Quinta:

## Top Recommendations:

**The Venue Sushi & Spirits Redefined (Palm Desert)**
The Venue Sushi & Spirits Redefined in Palm Desert stands as a "gem in the desert" with an ever-evolving menu crafted by owner/chef Engin Onural, earning it the title of "the BEST sushi restaurant in the valley." Patrons rave about the "amazing miso soup" and the unforgettable Omakase experience.

**Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar (La Quinta)**
Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar in La Quinta offers a truly exceptional Japanese Speciality experience, blending authentic Japanese flavors with a contemporary California twist. Okura is the only sushi restaurant in the region to receive daily deliveries of Grade A fish and seafood from Japan.

**Musashi Japanese Restaurant (Palm Desert)**
Musashi Japanese Restaurant in Palm Desert is cherished by locals and visitors alike for its "incredibly fresh sushi" and "outstanding service," with Executive Sushi Chef Masao Arai and Aki Akifumi masterfully crafting traditional Japanese flavors.

**Misaki Sushi & Grill (La Quinta)**
Misaki Sushi & Grill is a local sushi joint with some of the best, well-priced sushi in La Quinta, featuring an extensive menu that includes everything from classic rolls to their Sashimi Special.

**Otori Sushi (Palm Desert)**
Otori Sushi is a must-visit restaurant for sushi lovers in the Palm Desert with an extensive menu of traditional and specialty rolls.

These restaurants offer a range of price points and dining experiences, from casual to upscale, making it easy to find the perfect spot for any occasion!

input.name: Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar

reputation.review_volume: Yelp 1,400+ reviews; OpenTable ~2,979 reviews (4.7); Tripadvisor 251 reviews (4.1, #16 of 127 in La Quinta)
reputation.sentiment: Mostly positive — strong on OpenTable (4.7) and Yelp (~4.4); more mixed on Tripadvisor (4.1) with service-inconsistency complaints alongside food praise
reputation.recency_signal: Recent reviews (2025-2026) continue to praise sushi freshness, presentation, and ambiance (bronze Buddha, petrified cherry blossom tree); recurring criticisms note price-point ('expensive but worth it'), rushed service during peak season, and crowding
reputation.longevity: Opened in La Quinta in 2005; ~21 years operating under same ownership (Sam and Jay Lee); Laguna Beach sister location opened 2013 but Yelp now shows it as CLOSED
credentials: Desert Sun 'Best of the Valley' — Japanese restaurant category (multi-year winner, reported as 7-8+ consecutive years) [UNVERIFIED-exact-count]
Self-reported 'Best Sushi & Japanese in the Desert' (Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' / 2021 Best of Desert Awards claimed on own site) [UNVERIFIED-independent]

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: MIXED. Surfaces on broad 'best sushi La Quinta' and 'sushi near Palm Desert' aggregator-driven queries (OpenTable, Tripadvisor, resorthd.com). NOT mentioned in editorial 'best sushi Coachella Valley 2025' lists — Palm Springs Life's Asian roundup, Visit Palm Springs' best sushi list, and PS Tribune's 5 must-try Japanese spots all omit Okura, favoring Sandfish, Ponzu, Otori, Taka Shin, Musashi, Shabu Shabu Zen, Kobe Steakhouse. Loses the editorial conversation despite winning the local-paper popularity vote.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: When surfaced, descriptions are accurate — Japanese-California fusion, daily Grade A fish from Japan, distinctive decor, Highway 111 La Quinta location. Self-description and third-party descriptions align.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: okurasushi.com presents clean human-readable hours, address, phone, and menu sections, but no detectable JSON-LD / schema.org Restaurant markup surfaced via fetch. Site is e-commerce-enabled (online ordering, gift cards, reservations) but the structured signals an agent would parse cleanly (Restaurant schema, Menu schema, opening hours specification) appear absent.

competitors: Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey (Palm Springs) — chef-driven omakase, dominates editorial mentions
The Venue Sushi & Spirits Redefined (Palm Desert) — same chef-owner as Sandfish (Engin Onural)
Musashi Japanese Restaurant (Palm Desert) — chef-driven traditional
Misaki Sushi & Grill (La Quinta) — direct local sushi competitor at lower price point
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — long-standing Highway 111 La Quinta destination sushi anchor for 21 years; price-point and decor position it as the upscale incumbent in the east valley
query_wins: 'Japanese restaurant La Quinta' (Desert Sun Best of Valley legacy)

convertibility.ownership_signal: independent — owned by husband-and-wife Sam and Jay Lee since 2005 (Jay Lee listed as President on LinkedIn); contact email jleeokura@gmail.com is owner-direct, indicating reachable decision-maker
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Real branded domain (okurasushi.com) with online ordering, gift cards, and reservation integration; active Instagram (@okurasushi); Facebook presence (Okura Sushi La Quinta); OpenTable premium listing with 2,979+ reviews; paid/featured placement on playinlaquinta.com (city tourism board); presence on GAYOT, 10Best/USA Today, Grubhub, multiple aggregators — indicates ongoing marketing investment and listing maintenance, but no obvious agency/SEO footprint and no schema markup
convertibility.reasoning: Okura is a textbook convertible entity: independently owned, single operating location (Laguna Beach closed), 21-year operator with a reachable owner email, and clear evidence of self-directed marketing spend (own domain with commerce, OpenTable premium tier, social presence, tourism-board placement). The visibility gap is real and addressable — they win brand-search and aggregator surfaces but lose the editorial 'best sushi Coachella Valley' conversation to chef-driven competitors (Sandfish, The Venue, Musashi). Adding Restaurant schema, an awards/press page, and cultivating editorial coverage would directly close the gap without changing the product.

flags: [UNVERIFIED] Exact count of consecutive Desert Sun 'Best of the Valley' wins — sources cite '7 straight years' and '8+ years' inconsistently
[UNVERIFIED] Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' designation — claimed on own website but not confirmed via Palm Springs Life directly
[UNVERIFIED] '2021 Best of Desert Awards' — claimed on own site, not independently verified
[UNVERIFIED] Schema.org JSON-LD markup absence — WebFetch returns processed text not raw HTML; absence inferred but not directly observed in source
[UNVERIFIED] OpenTable Diners' Choice / Top 100 award status — OpenTable page timed out on fetch; 4.7/2,979 reviews confirmed via search snippet only
Sister location Okura Robata Laguna Beach listed as CLOSED on Yelp — La Quinta is now the sole operating location
